rules: History index is descriptive and bounded to the collections-ingest request chain.
The index may refer to save-slot-local artifacts and governing terminal app contracts.
The index does not create new write authority or widen path permissions.
Boards remain outside collections-ingest request history because Boards never use terminal/saves/.
guardrails: Consumers should enumerate request-chain artifacts from this index instead of guessing paths.
Trusted applied state still depends on repo-verified response content, not index presence alone.
The history index must stay read-oriented and bounded to the collections-ingest flow.
